```{r, echo=FALSE, results="hide"}
set.seed(sample.int(.Machine$integer.max, 1))

## Parâmetros aleatórios
mu     <- sample(c(4, 9, 16, 25), 1)
sigma2 <- sample(1:9, 1)

## g(x) = sqrt(x), g'(x) = 1/(2*sqrt(x))
## Variância assintótica = [g'(mu)]^2 * sigma2 = sigma2 / (4*mu)
correct <- round(sigma2 / (4 * mu), 4)

## Alternativas incorretas plausíveis
w1 <- round(sigma2 / (2 * mu), 4)         # usa 2 em vez de 4
w2 <- round(sigma2 * 4 * mu, 4)           # inverte
w3 <- round(sigma2 / (2 * sqrt(mu)), 4)   # usa sqrt(mu) incorretamente
w4 <- round(sigma2 / mu, 4)               # esquece o 4

## Garantir unicidade
w_list <- c(w1, w2, w3, w4)
for (i in seq_along(w_list)) {
  if (w_list[i] == correct) w_list[i] <- correct + 0.01
}

## Embaralhamento
all_vals <- c(correct, w_list[1], w_list[2], w_list[3], w_list[4])
# Garante cinco alternativas numericamente distintas após a parametrização.
integer_choices <- all(abs(all_vals - round(all_vals)) < sqrt(.Machine$double.eps))
choice_step <- if (integer_choices) 1 else max(1e-4, max(abs(all_vals), na.rm = TRUE) * 0.01)
while (anyDuplicated(all_vals)) {
  duplicate_position <- which(duplicated(all_vals))[1]
  all_vals[duplicate_position] <- max(all_vals, na.rm = TRUE) + choice_step
}
choices <- sample(all_vals)
sol_pos <- which(choices == correct)
sol_str <- paste(as.integer(seq_along(choices) == sol_pos), collapse = "")
```

Question
========

Seja $\bar{X}_n$ com variância assintótica $\sigma^2/n$ onde $\sigma^2 = `r sigma2`$. Considere a transformação $g(x) = \sqrt{x}$ com derivada $g'(x) = \dfrac{1}{2\sqrt{x}}$. Pelo Método Delta com $\mu = `r mu`$, a variância assintótica (em escala $\sqrt{n}$) de $g(\bar{X}_n) = \sqrt{\bar{X}_n}$ é:
$$[g'(\mu)]^2 \sigma^2 = \frac{\sigma^2}{4\mu}$$

Calcule $\sigma^2/(4\mu)$ para $\mu = `r mu`$ e $\sigma^2 = `r sigma2`$.

Answerlist
----------

* `r choices[1]`
* `r choices[2]`
* `r choices[3]`
* `r choices[4]`
* `r choices[5]`

Solution
========

Temos $g'(\mu) = \dfrac{1}{2\sqrt{\mu}} = \dfrac{1}{2\sqrt{`r mu`}} = \dfrac{1}{`r 2*sqrt(mu)`}$.

Portanto:
$$[g'(\mu)]^2 \sigma^2 = \left(\frac{1}{2\sqrt{`r mu`}}\right)^2 \times `r sigma2` = \frac{`r sigma2`}{4 \times `r mu`} = \frac{`r sigma2`}{`r 4*mu`} = `r correct`$$

Meta-information
================
exname: Cap2S2Q2_delta_sqrt
extype: schoice
exsolution: `r sol_str`
exshuffle: FALSE
```
